pattern: Index every value, then for each ordered pair treat them as the last two terms and walk backward through the chain with hashed lookups, counting the longest additive run.
hints: Two elements already determine the rest of an additive run, so think about pairs of indices as the state you build on.
Store each value's position in a map; for a pair ending at (i, j) the previous term would be arr[j] - arr[i], and if it exists earlier you can extend that pair's run by one.

Let dp[i][j] be the run length ending with arr[i], arr[j]. If arr[j]-arr[i] is a value at index k < i, set dp[i][j] = dp[k][i] + 1, else 2; track the maximum and report it only when it reached at least 3.

edge_inputs.description: consecutive integers hide a long additive run
several length-three runs but none longer
no additive triple exists at all so the answer is zero
minimum length that forms exactly one run
the canonical Fibonacci numbers form one long run
a near-Fibonacci array where one gap breaks the chain early
two disjoint runs of different lengths

stress.description: build a guaranteed long Fibonacci run and sprinkle decoy values that keep the array strictly increasing
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    a, b = rng.randint(1, 5), rng.randint(6, 12)
    fib = [a, b]
    while fib[-1] + fib[-2] < 5 * 10**8:
        fib.append(fib[-1] + fib[-2])
    base = set(fib)
    pool = set()
    x = fib[-1] + fib[-2] + 1
    while len(pool) < max(0, size - len(base)):
        if x not in base:
            pool.add(x)
        x += rng.randint(1, 7)
    arr = sorted(base | pool)
    return [arr]
stress.note: the embedded run guarantees a long answer; decoys never create a longer chain because they exceed every Fibonacci term
